summary: Browser automation CLI for JS-rendered pages, DOM inspection, interactive browser debugging, and browser-mediated `gather` fallback when private SaaS acquisition cannot stay on an official API/export path.

notes: Recommended install paths are npm, Homebrew, or Cargo.
Run `agent-browser install` after installing the CLI so Chrome for Testing or an existing browser runtime is available.

detect.failure_hint: Install the CLI first, then run `agent-browser install` to prepare a browser runtime.

healthcheck.failure_hint: The CLI exists but the runtime is not healthy. Run `python3 skills/support/agent-browser/scripts/runtime_guard.py --repo-root . --cleanup-orphans --execute` to remove orphan daemon trees, then reinstall or run `agent-browser install --with-deps` if the CLI itself is still unhealthy.

config_layers.summary: Prefer imported auth state, persistent profiles, session persistence, or auth-vault login when browser-mediated private-source gather already has approved local state.
Use origin-scoped headers only when the source contract explicitly allows header-based read access through the local browser runtime.
Some private SaaS flows need a one-time manual or headed bootstrap before later headless gather can continue honestly.

notes: Prefer the upstream support skill surface when the host runtime can consume it.
`charness` still owns the public `gather` fallback and degradation contract that decides when browser mediation is honest.

when_missing: Fall back to plain HTTP or non-browser workflows when possible.
For private SaaS gather, stop cleanly when browser auth/bootstrap is the missing boundary instead of pretending acquisition succeeded.
when_version_mismatch: Prefer operator guidance over silent retries when CLI behavior drifts.

host_notes: Hosts may need a separate Chromium or Chrome installation if they do not use `agent-browser install`.
Local desktop profile reuse is not equivalent to a remote or CI-like Linux runner; some flows require a one-time manual or headed bootstrap before later headless reuse is honest.
Long-lived orphan `agent-browser` daemons are treated as runtime drift, not as a healthy installed state.
